description: This figure displays process variants for producing hot-rolled (HR) and cold-rolled (CR) austenitic Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel strips, with 5 numbered process routes for different product requirements. The initial step is homogenization at 1100–1200°C, followed by hot rolling with finish rolling temperature (FRT) controlled at 900°C. For hot-rolled products: Route 1 adopts fast cooling to 500–750°C then slow cooling/isothermal holding; Route 2 uses fast cooling to room temperature followed by isothermal annealing, with cooling rates designed to avoid detrimental intergranular κ-carbide precipitation. For cold-rolled products: after cold rolling, Route 3 is 900–1100°C solution treatment followed by direct quenching; Routes 4 and 5 add 500–700°C aging for 5–20 h to maximize κ'-carbide precipitation hardening, while 600–900°C short-time recrystallization annealing is applied for non-age-hardenable grades to restore ductility. This figure summarizes optimized processing pathways to tune the microstructure and performance of the studied steel system.
